title: A Billion-Dollar Battle: Inside the Musk v. OpenAI Trial
subtitle: The world's richest man faces off against the creators of ChatGPT in a high-stakes legal showdown.

summary.en: Elon Musk is suing OpenAI, claiming the company broke its original promise to help humanity. He argues that the firm unfairly shifted to a for-profit model to make money. OpenAI denies these claims, suggesting Musk is simply jealous of their success. A federal judge is now overseeing the case, which features testimony from high-profile tech figures. The outcome could change how major AI companies are managed in the future.
summary.zh: 馬斯克正控告 OpenAI，聲稱該公司違反了造福人類的原始承諾。他主張該公司為了獲利，不公平地轉向營利模式。OpenAI 否認這些指控，暗示馬斯克只是嫉妒他們的成功。目前由聯邦法官審理此案，多位重量級科技人物已出庭作證。審判結果可能會改變未來大型人工智慧公司的管理方式。

paragraphs.en: A major legal battle is currently taking place in a California courtroom, pitting Elon Musk against the leaders of OpenAI. The case centers on a disagreement between the world’s richest man and the company behind ChatGPT, the famous artificial intelligence software. Musk, who helped start OpenAI in 2015, claims that CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman broke their founding agreement. He argues that they turned a non-profit organization into a for-profit business to gain personal wealth, moving away from their original goal of developing AI to benefit all of humanity.
paragraphs.zh: 一場重大的法律戰目前正在加州的法庭上演，由馬斯克（Elon Musk）對陣人工智慧研究公司 OpenAI 的領導層。此案的核心在於這位全球首富與這家開發知名人工智慧軟體 ChatGPT 的公司之間的分歧。馬斯克於二〇一五年協助創立 OpenAI，他指控執行長奧特曼（Sam Altman）與總裁布羅克曼（Greg Brockman）違反了當初的創立協議。他主張兩人為了獲取個人財富，將非營利組織轉變為營利企業，背離了開發人工智慧以造福全人類的初衷。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, Musk is seeking $134 billion in damages, which he wants to be returned to the company’s non-profit arm. He also wants Altman and Brockman removed from their leadership positions. However, OpenAI strongly rejects these accusations. The company’s lawyers argue that Musk is a "sore loser" who is trying to hurt a successful competitor to help his own AI startup, xAI. They claim that Musk was always aware of the plans to change the company’s structure and only turned against them after he failed to take control of the business in 2018.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，馬斯克要求一千三百四十億美元的損害賠償，並希望這筆錢能歸還給該公司的非營利部門。他也要求奧特曼與布羅克曼卸下領導職務。然而，OpenAI 強烈否認這些指控。該公司的律師辯稱，馬斯克只是個「輸不起的人」，試圖打擊成功的競爭對手，以協助他自己的人工智慧新創公司 xAI。他們聲稱，馬斯克一直知曉公司結構調整的計畫，直到二〇一八年他未能取得公司控制權後，才開始反對這些計畫。

paragraphs.en: Evidence presented in court, as reported by Ars Technica, shows that Musk actually tried to hire OpenAI’s founding team to work at Tesla years ago. Emails and text messages revealed that Musk had lost faith in the non-profit model by 2017 and wanted to build a powerful AI lab within his car company. These documents suggest that Musk was interested in commercializing AI technology himself, provided he was the one in charge. This has become a key point in the trial, as OpenAI’s lawyers use it to suggest that Musk’s lawsuit is not about charity, but about his own business interests.
paragraphs.zh: 根據科技媒體 Ars Technica 的報導，法庭上提出的證據顯示，馬斯克多年前曾試圖聘請 OpenAI 的創始團隊到特斯拉（Tesla）工作。電子郵件與簡訊內容揭露，馬斯克在二〇一七年時已對非營利模式失去信心，並希望在他的汽車公司內部建立一個強大的人工智慧實驗室。這些文件暗示，只要是由他本人主導，馬斯克其實很有興趣將人工智慧技術商業化。這已成為審判的關鍵點，OpenAI 的律師以此暗示馬斯克的訴訟並非為了慈善，而是為了他個人的商業利益。

paragraphs.en: One of the most anticipated witnesses in the trial was Shivon Zilis, an executive at Musk’s company Neuralink. Zilis served on the OpenAI board from 2020 to 2023 and shares four children with Musk. According to The Guardian, she acted as a link between Musk and the company after he left. During her testimony, she explained that she tried to help the different parties communicate better because they were struggling to get along. She also noted that she supported the move to a for-profit structure because it would help the company secure the massive investments needed to fulfill its mission.
paragraphs.zh: 審判中最受矚目的證人之一是馬斯克旗下公司 Neuralink 的高階主管齊里斯（Shivon Zilis）。齊里斯曾於二〇二〇年至二〇二三年擔任 OpenAI 董事，並與馬斯克育有四名子女。據《衛報》報導，她在馬斯克離開公司後，擔任他與公司之間的聯繫橋樑。在作證時，她解釋自己曾試圖協助各方進行更好的溝通，因為他們當時難以相處。她也提到，她支持轉向營利結構，因為這有助於公司獲得實現使命所需的巨額投資。

paragraphs.en: The trial is being overseen by U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers. Known for her strict and fair approach, she has kept tight control over the courtroom. According to BBC Business, she has not hesitated to correct Musk when he tried to act like his own lawyer during his testimony. Because the jury’s decision is only advisory, Judge Gonzalez Rogers will be the final person to decide the outcome of the case. This gives her significant power over the future of the companies involved.
paragraphs.zh: 此案由美國聯邦地方法院法官岡薩雷斯·羅傑斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers）審理。她以嚴格且公正的作風聞名，對法庭保持著嚴密的掌控。根據 BBC 商業新聞報導，當馬斯克在作證時試圖扮演自己的律師時，她毫不猶豫地糾正了他。由於陪審團的決定僅具諮詢性質，岡薩雷斯·羅傑斯法官將是決定案件結果的最終裁決者。這使她對相關企業的未來擁有重大影響力。

paragraphs.en: The case has also provided a rare look behind the scenes of the tech industry. For example, testimony has touched on the dramatic events of late 2023, when Sam Altman was briefly removed from his role as CEO. According to The Verge, witnesses and court documents have revealed new details about the internal power struggles that led to that moment, including concerns about Altman’s communication with the board regarding safety and business decisions.
paragraphs.zh: 此案也讓人們難得一窺科技產業的幕後情況。例如，證詞觸及了二〇二三年底發生的戲劇性事件，當時奧特曼曾短暫被免除執行長職務。根據《The Verge》報導，證人與法庭文件揭露了導致該事件的內部權力鬥爭細節，包括對奧特曼在安全與商業決策上與董事會溝通方式的擔憂。

paragraphs.en: As the trial continues, the tech world is watching closely. The outcome could have major implications for how AI companies are governed and whether they can legally shift from non-profit missions to profit-seeking businesses. While the jury is expected to share their thoughts by the end of the month, the final ruling will rest with the judge. For now, the courtroom remains a place where the world’s most powerful tech leaders must answer tough questions about their past actions and their visions for the future of artificial intelligence.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著審判持續進行，科技界正密切關注。審判結果可能對人工智慧公司的治理方式，以及它們是否能合法地從非營利使命轉向營利導向企業產生重大影響。雖然預計陪審團將在本月底前分享意見，但最終裁決仍將取決於法官。目前，法庭仍是全球最有權勢的科技領袖必須針對其過去行為及對人工智慧未來的願景，回答棘手問題的地方。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To set someone or something in conflict or competition against another.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（坑、果核），這裡作動詞，表示對立。文中：A major legal battle is currently taking place in a California courtroom, pitting Elon Musk against the leaders of OpenAI.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop supporting someone or something and start opposing them.
keyTerms.noteZh: turn 意為轉向，但加上 against 後意思完全相反，表示敵對。文中：They claim that Musk was always aware of the plans to change the company’s structure and only turned against them after he failed to take control of the business in 2018.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be the responsibility of someone or to be decided by someone.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 取決於某人；由某人負責。
keyTerms.noteZh: rest 常見作「休息」，這裡指權力或責任落在某人身上。文中：While the jury is expected to share their thoughts by the end of the month, the final ruling will rest with the judge.

sources.title: Elon Musk tried to hire OpenAI founders to start AI unit inside Tesla

quiz.question: What is the primary reason OpenAI's legal team claims Musk initiated this lawsuit?

options.A: He wants to return $134 billion to the non-profit arm to ensure ethical AI development.
options.B: He is attempting to undermine a rival company to benefit his own startup, xAI.
options.C: He was never informed about the company's transition to a for-profit structure.
options.D: He wants to reclaim his position as the CEO of OpenAI after being removed in 2018.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，OpenAI 的律師辯稱 Musk 是個「輸不起的人」（sore loser），目的是為了傷害競爭對手以幫助自己的 AI 新創公司 xAI。選項 A 雖然提到了 1340 億美元，但這是 Musk 的訴求，而非 OpenAI 律師指出的訴訟動機。選項 C 與文章內容矛盾，因為文中提到律師聲稱 Musk 一直知情。選項 D 錯誤，因為 Musk 是在 2018 年試圖奪取控制權失敗後才離開，而非被移除 CEO 職位。

quiz.question: Based on the evidence provided in the trial, what can be inferred about Musk's attitude toward the non-profit model?

options.A: He has always been a firm believer that AI must remain a non-profit to be safe.
options.B: He only supported the non-profit structure as long as he was the one in control of it.
options.C: He was unaware that the non-profit model would limit the company's ability to raise funds.
options.D: He intentionally sabotaged the company in 2017 to force it to become for-profit.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第三段，證據顯示 Musk 在 2017 年就對非營利模式失去信心，並試圖將 OpenAI 的團隊轉移到特斯拉，這暗示他對非營利模式的堅持取決於他是否能掌控該技術的商業化。選項 A 與證據矛盾。選項 C 無法從文中推論，因為 Musk 本身就是想商業化的人。選項 D 過度推論，文中僅提到他想將技術帶入自己的公司，而非刻意破壞 OpenAI。

quiz.question: In the context of the second paragraph, what does the phrase 'sore loser' imply about Musk?

options.A: He is physically injured from a competition.
options.B: He is complaining because he failed to achieve his desired outcome.
options.C: He is a person who has lost a lot of money in the stock market.
options.D: He is someone who is naturally bad at playing games.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。「Sore loser」是一個慣用語，指某人在比賽或競爭中失敗後，表現出憤怒、不滿或指責他人，無法坦然接受失敗。在文中，OpenAI 的律師用此詞形容 Musk，是因為他無法接受在 2018 年奪權失敗的事實，因此現在透過法律手段進行報復。選項 A 是字面錯誤理解；選項 C 與 D 雖然提到失敗或競爭，但未精確捕捉到該詞彙在「抱怨失敗者」這一層面的語意。

quiz.question: What is the central focus of the legal battle between Elon Musk and OpenAI?

options.A: A personal dispute regarding the custody of children shared between Musk and an executive.
options.B: A debate over whether Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers is qualified to oversee tech cases.
options.C: A conflict regarding the shift of OpenAI from a non-profit mission to a for-profit business.
options.D: A technical disagreement about the safety protocols of ChatGPT software.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。整篇文章的核心都在討論 Musk 指控 OpenAI 將非營利組織轉變為營利企業，違背了造福人類的初衷，並探討了此舉背後的動機與影響。選項 A 雖然在第四段提到，但只是為了背景說明，非訴訟核心。選項 B 僅是關於法官的背景描述，非案件主旨。選項 D 雖然在第六段提到 Altman 與董事會溝通時涉及安全議題，但這只是案件中揭露的細節之一，並非訴訟的主要爭議點。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
